snippet.title: Race and Mormon Scripture Pt. 1 | Ep. 1663 | LDS Discussions Ep. 21
snippet.description:
Join Mike from LDS discussions.com and John as they discuss race and Mormon scripture. This is part 1 of a 2-part series. We are also grateful to have a few call-in guests for this episode. 

Today’s episode covers:
-Personal stories from black members of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (Mormons) of the racist things said to them and ways they were treated, and how psychologically harmful the racist doctrines have been for them
-The most problematic verses and teaching of past prophets from The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints that specifically highlight dark or black skin as a curse directly from God 
-Context and proof showing the belief literally meant dark skin and was not an idiom or euphemism for anything else
-The early belief that the skin color of Indigenous North Americans could/did actually change to become lighter when converting to Mormonism and living more “righteously”
-The Book of Abraham as evidence of Joseph Smith’s racism, codifying racism into scripture for ALL black people, not just as a curse specific to North American Indigenous people as stated in the Book of Mormon.
-The doctrinal basis for the early Mormon ban against black people worshiping and marrying in the Temple, as well as the ban against black Mormon men from receiving the Priesthood, the authority needed to hold any leadership position in the Church as well as perform essential saving ordinances such as baptism, receiving the gift of the Holy Ghost etc.
-The real reasons the Church was pressured to lift the ban and the measures taken to make it happen despite continued vehement opposition among some Church leaders.

LDS Discussions Essay: https://www.ldsdiscussions.com/race

LDS Discussions Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6p7gAxwsM_k&list=PLxq5opj6GqOB7J1n6pMmdUSezxcLfsced 

Chapters:
00:00:00 Introduction
00:06:00 Building on previous episodes to look at race and LDS scriptures
00:09:40 Race and the Book of Mormon
00:16:00 Taking the text of the Book of Mormon at face value
00:21:00 Dark skin is absolutely a curse from God in the Book of Mormon
00:22:50 The church absolutely believed skin meant skin
00:25:50 Missed in Sunday on BOM race changes
00:27:00 Appearance by Sabrina to comment on depiction of Jesus as white
00:31:10 This is a view you would expect from Joseph Smith in the Book of Mormon
00:36:50 Quotes from church leaders confirming the Book of Mormon
00:41:00 A Mormon prophet describes skin color at General Conference
00:44:40 More church leaders on the curse of dark skin
00:46:20 What the Mormon church told Native Americans and Pollynesians
00:49:30 Testable truth claims made by the Book of Mormon
00:58:30 The 2020 Come Follow Me manual uproar
01:00:10 Here’s the full quote of what the Come Follow Me manual cites
01:05:00 Curse of black skin in the Book of Moses
01:08:00 Channel shares her experiences with racism in the Church and internalizing it.
01:21:55 The priesthood ban written into the Book of Abraham
01:26:40 Joseph Smith mistakes Anubis for a slave…because he is black
01:28:00 Joseph even altered the woodcut to make Anubis a slave
01:30:10 How the church has changed the Book of Mormon to fix SOME of the racist language
01:32:50 All the prophets believed in slavery
01:35:20 The Mormon church called the priesthood ban doctrine
01:40:25 The Priesthood Ban was not just a Priesthood Ban
01:45:00 The lifting of the Priesthood ban
01:46:20 How and why the ban was actually lifted
01:48:30 The LaGrand Richard interview, continued
01:50:40 Takeaways of why the ban was lifted 
01:58:30 Implications of the lifting of the Priesthood and temple ban
02:02:10 Closing remarks from Sabrina and Channel

MIRACULOUS - TALES OF LADYBUG & CAT NOIR OFFICIAL YOUTUBE CHANNEL
Two high-school students, Marinette and Adrien, are chosen to become Paris’ superheroes: Ladybug and Cat Noir. They each have a Miraculous, a jewel linked to the magical creatures (Kwamis) that give them powers.
Their mission is to keep Hawk Moth – who wants to steal their Miraculouses – from wreaking havoc on the town by creating ever fiercer supervillains – all while living their high school lives and keeping their identities secret.
At school, Marinette has a crush on Adrien but cannot tell him, so she acts awkwardly whenever he is around. As a superhero, Cat Noir loves Ladybug, but his ego annoys her: besides, Marinette prefers Adrien anyway!
Throughout the school year and their adventures, Ladybug discovers many nice qualities in Cat Noir while Marinette grows ever closer to Adrien, making huge efforts to overcome her shyness.

The adventure in Paris continues for Ladybug and Cat Noir, surrounded by new allies and fighting an ever more powerful and determined opponent. But great changes lie ahead for Ladybug and Cat Noir in their superhero lives, and for Marinette and Adrien in their lives at school.

snippet.title: 🎀 BEAUTIFUL Shell Stitch Crochet Baby Blanket Pattern (1 Row Repeat Only!) 🤩
snippet.description:
📌Buy the PDF Pattern on Etsy: https://maisieandruth.com/shellblanket

This shell stitch crochet baby blanket pattern is a quick and easy 1-row repeat for you! You can make this stunning baby blanket by following along with my detailed, step-by-step tutorial (US crochet terms used).

🎀 FREE WRITTEN PATTERN:
https://maisieandruth.com/shell-crochet-baby-blanket-free-pattern/

====================

🌟 Set the video to 1080p (for best quality) 
🌎 Subtitles available (select the language of your preference)
❤️ If you liked this video, please SUBSCRIBE, SHARE and LIKE!

====================

BEGINNERS: Remember that you can adjust the speed of the video in the settings tab. If it’s too fast, you can always slow it down even further!

====================

⏰  TIMESTAMPS (Jump to the section you need immediately):
00:00   Introduction 
01:53   Materials
02:41   Row 1
16:45   Row 2  

====================

This blanket is reversible. The finished size is 26 x 26” (66 x 66cm).

🎀 The tutorial for the 3D bow is here:
https://youtu.be/ptCnTLD0Io8

This is an easy crochet tutorial and I’ll show you what to do step by step. You will need to be familiar with the following stitches: 
ch(s) - chain(s) 
dc – double crochet
FPsc  - front post single crochet
sc - single crochet 
sk – skip

====================

🎀 MATERIALS USED (AFFILIATE LINKS): 
🔹Yarn: #3 / light worsted / DK / 8 ply yarn. You'll need 1045m / 1143yds. (I used 5 balls of Sirdar Snuggly Soothing DK, 241yds / 3.5 oz [220m / 100g]) https://tinyurl.com/6kj6wd4c
ALTERNATIVE YARN SUGGESTIONS:
1. Premier Basix DK Yarn: https://www.marymaxim.com/collections/yarn/products/premier-basix-dk-yarn 
2. Cascade 220 Superwash Yarn: https://www.jimmybeanswool.com/knitting/yarn/Cascade/220Superwash.asp 
3. Paintbox Yarns Simply DK: https://tinyurl.com/y79f5wff 
4. Stylecraft Special DK: https://tinyurl.com/eajwc7de 

🔹Crochet hook: Size 7 (4.5mm) https://amzn.to/3hxS081
🔹Scissors https://amzn.to/3vn9Ori
🔹Yarn needle https://amzn.to/3fpjtbw

====================

snippet.title: “Take opportunities” Meet Wafa – Executive Assistant | AXA
snippet.description:
What does it mean to Grow Your Potential at AXA? 🌱
For Wafa, Executive Assistant to AXA Global Head of Internal Audit, it means taking opportunities. After 15 years working at AXA, she had the opportunity to work abroad in the US and Indonesia. These experiences allowed her to grow, learn and understand others better. 

Her advice "listen to your heart and speak up with a voice and you will really have the perfect opportunity".

🔸 Grow your potential: what does it mean to us?
We know how much our people are capable of. That’s why we make sure we provide the right environment, learning tools and encouragement to seize every opportunity and rise to every challenge. We see your potential. 

🔸How are we helping you build the career you’ve always wanted?
📈 Offering our employees stimulating career opportunities within or across functions and countries. Did you know that in 2021, 41% of our vacancies have been filled by internal employees? Discover how far you will go with us!

🎓 Providing them with access to top notch learning programs and courses. Thanks to a partnership with LinkedIn Learning, our employees benefit from over 20,000 courses available in 7 different languages.

snippet.title: Chris Hemsworth’s Favorite Vacation Spot
snippet.description:
Chris Hemsworth's Favorite Vacation Spot in Australia

Chris Hemsworth Best Motivation 15:55

1. Lord Howe Island, NSW
“It’s beautiful, with incredible surf and not too crowded – but maybe that won’t be the case now that I’ve talked it up!”
2. Great Barrier Reef, Qld
“I went scuba diving on the reef for the first time which was amazing. There is more sea life and vibrant colours of coral than I’ve ever seen. It’s like visiting another planet. Flying over the reef and seeing the changing blues and greens was incredible. Such intense colours and contrast to the white sand.”
3. The Kimberley, WA
“We went hiking and fishing for a day [in a location so remote they had to be choppered in] and caught a lot of barramundi,” says Hemsworth, adding he and his mates were on the watch for crocodiles constantly.
“Trying to catch a fish while looking in a 360-degree loop is a bit tricky.”
4. Cape Woolamai, Phillip Island, Vic
“One of my favourite spots to surf. I spent a lot of my childhood there and have great memories growing up with my brothers.”
5. Mallacoota, East Gippsland, Vic
“This place is responsible for my love of the ocean. I camped here every Christmas with my family and it has everything; white sandy beaches, crystal clear water, great surf and an abundance of wildlife.”
6. Cape Schanck, Mornington Peninsula, Vic
“Raw and rugged coastline paired with some of the most incredible bushwalks you’ll ever experience.”
7. Currumbin Alley, Gold Coast, Qld
“Perfect for families, it’s where a waist-deep,crystal-clear coastal estuary meets the ocean. This beach is kid heaven.”
8. Julian Rocks, Byron Bay, NSW
“It’s a protected marine park so there’s an abundance of sea life for divers.”
9. Whitehaven Beach, The Whitsundays, Qld
“Absolutely stunning. The most pristine white sand, crystal clean water. The perfect spot to just unwind, surrounded by the reef.”

****

“The very first sentence of the White Paper says that Bitcoin is a peer to peer electronic cash system that can allow people to send payments without going through financial institutions. That’s the first sentence: built around self custody, not around inflation protection, not around digital gold.”
— Nick Neuman

Location: New York
Date: Thursday 9th December
Company: Casa
Role: CEO

Not your keys, not your coins. 

Despite being a central mantra of Bitcoiners, self-custody is still an issue for the ecosystem. Whether it be major institutional investors or new small volume retail buyers, there are material concerns over the risks and technical skills required with being responsible for Bitcoin keys.  

Whilst delegating custody removes perceived barriers to entry, they present new risks to individuals and the wider network. These new risks may be nebulous and abstract to many. But they are real and significant issues that contravene Satoshi’s central mission for a decentralized self-sovereign system of peer to peer cash.

The paradox is that rather than requiring specialist technical skills, modern hardware wallets are both significantly more user friendly and intuitive than their recent predecessors. “The very first sentence of the White Paper says that Bitcoin is a peer to peer electronic cash system that can allow people to send payments without going through financial institutions. That’s the first sentence: built around self custody, not around inflation protection, not around digital gold.”
— Nick Neuman

Location: New York
Date: Thursday 9th December
Company: Casa
Role: CEO

Not your keys, not your coins. 

Despite being a central mantra of Bitcoiners, self-custody is still an issue for the ecosystem. Whether it be major institutional investors or new small volume retail buyers, there are material concerns over the risks and technical skills required with being responsible for Bitcoin keys.  

Whilst delegating custody removes perceived barriers to entry, they present new risks to individuals and the wider network. These new risks may be nebulous and abstract to many. But they are real and significant issues that contravene Satoshi’s central mission for a decentralized self-sovereign system of peer to peer cash.

The paradox is that rather than requiring specialist technical skills, modern hardware wallets are both significantly more user friendly and intuitive than their recent predecessors. This is both in terms of the products themselves and the support infrastructure (i.e. helplines etc.) provided by manufacturers. 

Whilst it may be easy to have someone else store your bitcoin, it is still the case that if you don’t own the keys, you don’t control the coins. The irony is you may only discover this at the time you are in most need of your bitcoin.

In this interview, I talk to Nick Neuman, CEO of Casa. We discuss the scale of third-party custodying of bitcoin, the inherent risks of delegating bitcoin custody, the security and support provided to users of hardware wallets, and the latest innovations.

snippet.title: Three tips for starting with purées for baby from a pediatric dietitian
snippet.description:
Welcome to video #8 (out of 21) inside Feeding Baby 101 -- a FREE course for parents all about introducing baby to their first foods using science based methods that support healthy relationships with food!

Hi 👋  I'm Dr. Taylor and I am so glad you are here!  If you are just joining in, please head to the course homepage (playlist) and start from the beginning.  And don't forget to scroll down for some key points from this video and your homework.

FEEDING BABY 101 (FREE COURSE playlist here👇)
https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLaleJGmG5SPk0E6-kRm5LP1Rnk3dsem9O

FEEDING BABY 101 ebook 📚👇
https://www.patreon.com/posts/feeding-baby-101-70710005?utm_medium=clipboard_copy&utm_source=copyLink&utm_campaign=postshare_creator&utm_content=join_link

Key Points:
• Waiting until your baby is developmentally ready to start solid foods (solid foods include purées!)
• Use a baby led feeding approach
• Focus on purées as a transitional food
• Transitioning to lumpy foods before 10 months (and ideally earlier) is important to prevent texture aversions
• Purées can be a great addition to any baby meal, especially as a kcal (Calorie) booster, a sauce, or a dip
• Pouches: a great tool to use, but try not to rely on them all the time
• Babies fed purées do not have a statistically lower risk for choking than babies fed soft, whole table foods (#science)
• The way you feed your baby is not a reflection of your parenting abilities ❤️

Homework:

localized.description:
Welcome to video #8 (out of 21) inside Feeding Baby 101 -- a FREE course for parents all about introducing baby to their first foods using science based methods that support healthy relationships with food!

Hi 👋  I'm Dr. Taylor and I am so glad you are here!  If you are just joining in, please head to the course homepage (playlist) and start from the beginning.  And don't forget to scroll down for some key points from this video and your homework.

Key Points:
• Waiting until your baby is developmentally ready to start solid foods (solid foods include purées!)
• Use a baby led feeding approach
• Focus on purées as a transitional food
• Transitioning to lumpy foods before 10 months (and ideally earlier) is important to prevent texture aversions
• Purées can be a great addition to any baby meal, especially as a kcal (Calorie) booster, a sauce, or a dip
• Pouches: a great tool to use, but try not to rely on them all the time
• Babies fed purées do not have a statistically lower risk for choking than babies fed soft, whole table foods (#science)
• The way you feed your baby is not a reflection of your parenting abilities ❤️

Homework:
• If you choose to start with purees, evaluate where you can add texture.  For example, cottage cheese and guacamole are GREAT ways to introduce lumpy textures early.

snippet.title: 1969 Pontiac Trans AM for sale at Volo Auto Museum (V20336)
snippet.description:
For more information on this vehicle and to see over 100 pictures, follow the link below or click SHOW MORE.
https://www.volocars.com/auto-sales/vehicles/17207/1969-pontiac-trans-am

1 of 634 Trans Ams built total in 1969! This one is #s matching and documented by Pontiac Historical Services. It underwent a 1,500 hour body off frame restoration at a professional shop. I just acquired it from a private collection deep in the Georgia mountains.

EXTERIOR: The fit and finish is excellent. There is no rust or damage. It's straight every where, all the panels and pieces fit properly. It has functional ram air hood, fender air extractors and a rear spoiler which is unique to just the 69 T/A. The Cameo white paint with blue racing stripes is beautiful. It has a smooth and glossy finish. I did not find any chips, cracks or scratches. The front end bumpersand bezels fit real nice. The chrome has been restored or replaced. Windshield was replaced. The rally ll wheels are restored, it has correct lug nuts and center caps. Reproduction Polyglass tires.

INTERIOR: Restored from carpet to headliner. Cushions were replaced, upholstery fits tight. The dash area is restored, it's not cut or broken. Mint condition deluxe wheel. Headliner fits tight. Complete set of original seatbelts. The best part is the 4-speed sticking out of the floor!

ENGINE COMPARTMENT: #s matching Ram Air lll 400. The engine bay is restored to factory condition. The ram air system is complete. Stock manifolds, valve covers, carb, distributor etc. It features power steering and power brakes. It has reproduction hoses and clamps. It has a Delco battery topper with spring clamp cables. All the metal finishes are smooth and painted nicely.

TRUNK COMPARTMENT: Clean spatter painted metal. Under the trunk lid and the jamb are painted glossy. Matching rally Il wheel and reproduction Polyglass tire for a spare. Jack assembly is restored. The mat, weatherstrip and decal were replaced.

UNDERSIDE: The body was off the frame, every component was removed, rebuilt and restored. It even has assembly line markings reapplied. It has the ride and handling package.
The front end was completely rebuilt- the bushings, ball joints, springs, shocks and steering components. The rear suspension also got new bushings and shocks. The brake system was completely restored, it has disc in front. The brake lines, hoses and e-brake assembly were replaced. It has an authentic exhaust system with 2 mufflers and a transverse muffler. The fuel system was replaced. Bottom of the motor and starter look clean. The vin # matching Muncie M20 4-speed looks fresh.

35 years of Trans Am production and the 1969 stands out. It was a very limited production, 1 year design. I'm in the business and rarely see a real one. I am very excited to have this car in inventory. Don't miss your chance to own an exciting investment!

I, Jay Grams, take pride in personally writing the descriptions for virtually every car for about the last 30 years now. A significant amount of effort goes into fact checking. What I am offering is my professional impression of the vehicle.
Our salesmen are happy to assist with descriptions and making sure your questions are answered. One great suggestion is letting us do a walk around video that explains the car's condition in detail.
**These cars are displayed in a museum. If interested in a particular car make an appointment with a salesman to see that car. Without an appointment there is an admission fee to enter the showroom. If you buy a car, your admission will be refunded and you will be given a 1 year membership.

Visit our website to see over 300 vehicles for sale!
